patch.target: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
patch.before: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
patch.after: If your brand isn't being quoted by ChatGPT, you don't exist to half your buyers. That's what AEO fixes—and why splitting it from GEO is burning budget.

Specific human voice vs committee 2/10 — Reads like a templated SEO brief with reviewer bylines, 'Quick answer' blocks, and 'Key takeaways' scaffolding—no human cadence.

suggestedPatch.target: This glossary guide defines AEO for 2026, explains why the lines with GEO and LLMO have disappeared, provides real audit data and brand examples, and offers a practical table to compare each approach.
suggestedPatch.before: This glossary guide defines AEO for 2026, explains why the lines with GEO and LLMO have disappeared, provides real audit data and brand examples, and offers a practical table to compare each approach.
suggestedPatch.after: I'll show you why AEO, GEO, and LLMO have collapsed into one discipline—and share audit data from 1,240 SaaS brands that convinced me.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Specific human voice vs committee" scored 2/10: Reads like a templated SEO brief with reviewer bylines, 'Quick answer' blocks, and 'Key takeaways' scaffolding—no human cadence.

findings.evidence: Genuine opinions vs safe claims 4/10 — There is a stated 'take' but it's hedged and repeated verbatim across sections rather than argued with conviction.

suggestedPatch.target: AEO, GEO, and LLMO are converging into a single discipline — treating them as separate strategies is wrong and wastes budget
suggestedPatch.before: AEO, GEO, and LLMO are converging into a single discipline — treating them as separate strategies is wrong and wastes budget
suggestedPatch.after: Running separate AEO, GEO, and LLMO teams is the dumbest budget line item I've seen in 2026—kill it before your CFO does.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Genuine opinions vs safe claims" scored 4/10: There is a stated 'take' but it's hedged and repeated verbatim across sections rather than argued with conviction.

findings.evidence: Unexpected phrasings 2/10 — Every sentence leans on predictable SEO-blog phrasing: 'fragment your signal,' 'holistic strategy,' 'drive visibility.'

suggestedPatch.target: Brands that spread resources across separate playbooks fragment their signal and dilute performance.
suggestedPatch.before: Brands that spread resources across separate playbooks fragment their signal and dilute performance.
suggestedPatch.after: Split your playbook across three acronyms and you'll end up with three half-baked strategies and zero citations.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Unexpected phrasings" scored 2/10: Every sentence leans on predictable SEO-blog phrasing: 'fragment your signal,' 'holistic strategy,' 'drive visibility.'

findings.evidence: Point of view vs both-sides survey 6/10 — Has a thesis (convergence) but delivers it in surveying, encyclopedia-style prose rather than argument.

suggestedPatch.target: Originally, Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and Large Language Model Optimization (LLMO) emerged to describe nuances of optimizing for ChatGPT and similar LLMs.
suggestedPatch.before: Originally, Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) and Large Language Model Optimization (LLMO) emerged to describe nuances of optimizing for ChatGPT and similar LLMs.
suggestedPatch.after: GEO and LLMO were vendor-invented acronyms that never described anything AEO didn't already cover—the convergence isn't new, the labels were redundant.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Point of view vs both-sides survey" scored 6/10: Has a thesis (convergence) but delivers it in surveying, encyclopedia-style prose rather than argument.

findings.evidence: Quotable sentence 2/10 — Nothing here is memorable; it's all generic optimization advice phrased in passive declarative form.

suggestedPatch.target: Engines reward brands with centralized, schema-driven content and original data, regardless of engine label.
suggestedPatch.before: Engines reward brands with centralized, schema-driven content and original data, regardless of engine label.
suggestedPatch.after: The engines don't care which acronym is on your Jira ticket—they reward the same four signals every time.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Quotable sentence" scored 2/10: Nothing here is memorable; it's all generic optimization advice phrased in passive declarative form.

findings.evidence: Intro earns attention in first 2 sentences 3/10 — Opens with a dictionary definition and an acronym list—classic featured-snippet bait that doesn't pull the reader in.

suggestedPatch.target: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.before: AEO—answer engine optimization—refers to improving brand visibility in AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, and Perplexity.
suggestedPatch.after: Only 11% of B2B SaaS brands show up in a ChatGPT buyer prompt they weren't named in. AEO is how the other 89% fix that.
suggestedPatch.rationale: Humanization axis "Intro earns attention in first 2 sentences" scored 3/10: Opens with a dictionary definition and an acronym list—classic featured-snippet bait that doesn't pull the reader in.

rewritesApplied.before: Splitwise dominates share of voice for ‘split group expenses’ across Perplexity and ChatGPT, driven by robust references on Wikipedia/Reddit rather than through direct AEO investment.
rewritesApplied.after: Splitwise owns most of the conversation around "split group expenses" on Perplexity and ChatGPT, but that visibility comes from heavy mentions on Wikipedia and Reddit, not from any deliberate AEO strategy.
rewritesApplied.rationale: Remove AI-signature word "robust" via targeted resentence

suggestedPatch.after: If your brand isn't being quoted by ChatGPT, you don't exist to half your buyers. That's what AEO fixes—and why splitting it from GEO is burning budget.
